title: calochortus syntrophus
intro: Calochortus syntrophus, also known as the desert mariposa lily or mariposa cat's eye, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to California and Baja California. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Calochortus syntrophus include its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help in relieving congestion and opening up airways. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the flowers and using them in teas or tinctures, although due to its endangered status, commercial cultivation and use are highly regulated.
